—Israel mourned for the Benjamites and was troubled by the oath they had taken, not to give their daughters to them in marriage. People are more eager to defend their own power than that of God. They would have acted better if they had repented of their hasty oaths, brought sin-offerings, and sought forgiveness in the appointed way, rather than try to avoid the guilt of perjury by actions just as wrong. That people can advise others to acts of betrayal or violence, out of a sense of duty, shows strongly how blind the human mind is when left to itself, and the dangerous effects of a conscience under ignorance and error.
